title: Remark 1.2 (Open Problem on Sparse Densities)
statement_text: The problem of finding the asymptotic rate across the whole range of sparse densities remains open.
context_text: Theorem 1.1 establishes the asymptotic rate for moderate deviations of subgraph counts in $G(n,m)$ when $t(n)$ is bounded away from 1, with a partial extension to sparser regimes satisfying $\max\{t^{1/2}n^{-1/2}\log n,\, t^{e-3/2}\} \ll \alpha_n \ll \min\{t^{2e-5/2}n^{1/2},\, t^{e+2}n^{1/2}\}$. The authors note they initially proved results only in the dense case $t\in(0,1)$ constant and have only partially extended to sparser regimes.
notes: Stated in Remark 1.2 as an open problem in prose form, without a dedicated labeled theorem environment. PDF source — math notation in surrounding theorems may be partially garbled.
paper_title: Moderate deviations of subgraph counts in the Erdős-Rényi random graphs $G(n,m)$ and $G(n,p)$
